tasks.spec:
You are a Ringer worker creating ONE new Python file in a git worktree of the ringer repo. Your current working directory IS that worktree — create the file here, leave ALL changes uncommitted (NEVER run git commit; the check exports your diff as a patch). You own EXACTLY ONE new file: scripts/transcript.py. Do NOT modify any other file. Python 3.11+, standard library ONLY (json, re, os, pathlib — no third-party imports).

GOAL: parse a raw Ringer worker.log into a readable, turn-by-turn TRANSCRIPT of the conversation between the orchestrator (who sent the spec) and the worker (an OpenCode agent). This feeds a live UI, so correctness and robustness matter more than cleverness.

PUBLIC API (exact — a check imports these):
  def parse_transcript(log_text: str, *, task: dict | None = None, max_bytes: int = 2_000_000) -> dict
  def parse_transcript_file(path, *, task: dict | None = None, max_bytes: int = 2_000_000) -> dict
parse_transcript_file reads the file (tail to max_bytes if larger) and calls parse_transcript.

RETURN SHAPE (exact keys):
{
  'schema': 1,
  'engine': '<opencode|mock|unknown>',        # from the '[ringer.py] engine:' line
  'sessions': ['ses_...'],                      # distinct real session ids, first-seen order
  'status': 'success'|'failed'|'running',       # the LAST attempt's outcome
  'attempts': [ {
      'n': 1, 'started_at': '<iso from the started marker>',
      'session_id': 'ses_...'|None,             # this attempt's OWN session (from its real events)
      'rc': 0|1|None,                            # last real 'exited rc=' in the segment; None if none
      'outcome': 'success'|'failed'|'running',
      'turns': [ ...see below... ],
      'tokens_final': {..}|None,                 # 'tokens' block of the LAST step_finish in the attempt
      'cost': 0 } ],
  'tokens_total': <int>,                         # tokens_final.total of the LAST attempt (0 if none)
  'parse_warnings': [ '<str>' ]
}
TURN objects:
  orchestrator spec:   {'role':'orchestrator','kind':'spec','text': <base spec prompt>}
  orchestrator reply:  {'role':'orchestrator','kind':'retry_reply','text': <injected failure context>}
  worker text:         {'role':'worker','kind':'text','text':..,'session_id':..,'t_start':..,'t_end':..}
  worker tool:         {'role':'worker','kind':'tool','tool':..,'input':<obj>,'output':<str>,'exit':<int|None>,'ok':<bool>,'call_id':..,'session_id':..,'t_start':..,'t_end':..}
  worker step:         {'role':'worker','kind':'step','reason':..,'tokens':<obj>,'cost':..,'session_id':..}
  worker error:        {'role':'worker','kind':'error','message':..,'name':..,'session_id':..}

WORKER.LOG FORMAT (study this carefully — the retry case is the trap):
A log is one or more ATTEMPTS concatenated. Each attempt is:
  [ringer.py] attempt N started <iso>
  [ringer.py] engine: <opencode|mock>
  [ringer.py] command: <shell cmdline>            <- may span MANY physical lines
  <NDJSON event lines, one JSON object per line>  <- the real worker output
  [ringer.py] attempt N exited rc=<int>
The 'command:' line's LAST argument is the SPEC PROMPT, wrapped in SINGLE QUOTES, and it can contain literal newlines (so the command spans multiple physical lines). The real command always ends with the tokens `< /dev/null` AFTER the closing quote (a baked-in invariant). Inside that single-quoted arg, any literal single-quote is shell-escaped as the 5-char sequence '\''"'"'\'' (i.e. quote, double-quote, quote, double-quote, quote) — unescape each such run back to a single '.

THE RETRY TRAP (must handle): on attempt 2+, the spec arg has, appended after the base spec, the text 'Previous attempt failed: ' followed by a FULL REPRODUCTION of the previous attempt's log — including reproduced '[ringer.py] attempt/engine/command' lines AND reproduced NDJSON '{"type":...}' event lines, all still INSIDE the single-quoted arg. You MUST NOT treat any of that embedded reproduction as real markers or real events. Only the events AFTER the real command's closing-quote+`< /dev/null` are this attempt's real events.

RECOMMENDED ALGORITHM:
1. If len(log_text) > max_bytes, keep the last max_bytes and add a parse_warnings note.
2. Segment into attempts by scanning for lines matching, at START OF LINE, the regex ^\[ringer\.py\] attempt (\d+) started (.*)$ . (The embedded reproduction's 'Previous attempt failed: [ringer.py] attempt 1 started' is NOT at start-of-line as a bare '[ringer.py]' — it is prefixed by 'Previous attempt failed: ' — so it will not match. Good.) Each match begins a new segment; capture n and the iso.
3. Within a segment, find the '[ringer.py] command: ' line. Take everything from just after 'command: ' to the END of the segment as one big string `blob`. Walk `blob` with a SINGLE-QUOTE SHELL STATE MACHINE: find the first single-quote (the arg opener), then scan forward; whenever you see the 5-char escape run '\''"'"'\'' treat it as a literal ' and continue INSIDE the quote; a lone ' that is NOT part of that escape run CLOSES the arg. The text you accumulated (with escapes turned back into ') is the SPEC PROMPT. The index right after the closing quote marks where the real command tail (` < /dev/null`) and then the REAL NDJSON begin. Everything in `blob` after the closing quote is the real-events region for this attempt.
4. Determine engine from the 'engine:' line. If engine is not opencode (e.g. 'mock'), there is no NDJSON: emit the spec turn, and emit any non-empty plain-text lines of the real-events region as a single worker 'text' turn (joined). outcome from rc.
5. For opencode: split the real-events region into lines; for each line that starts with '{', json.loads inside try/except (on failure: increment a parse_warnings counter, skip the line — a truncated final line must NOT crash you). Map by top-level 'type':
   - 'step_start'  -> ignore (structural only); optionally track part.messageID.
   - 'text'        -> worker text turn (part.text, part.time.start/end -> t_start/t_end, top-level sessionID -> session_id).
   - 'tool_use'    -> worker tool turn: part.tool, part.callID->call_id, part.state.input->input, part.state.output->output, part.state.metadata.exit->exit, ok = (part.state.status == 'completed'), part.state.time.start/end. TRUNCATE input/output string fields to ~4000 chars each with an ellipsis note. sessionID->session_id.
   - 'step_finish' -> worker step turn: part.reason, part.tokens->tokens, part.cost->cost, sessionID. Update the attempt's tokens_final to this event's part.tokens.
   - 'error'       -> worker error turn: error.data.message (fallback error.name)->message, error.name->name, sessionID. Mark the attempt failed.
6. The base spec vs retry_reply: for attempt n==1 emit an orchestrator 'spec' turn (prefer task['spec'] if task is given, else the parsed spec prompt). For n>1: if the parsed spec prompt contains 'Previous attempt failed:', split on the FIRST occurrence — the text BEFORE is the (already-shown) base spec so DROP it, and emit the text AFTER as an orchestrator 'retry_reply' turn. If there is no such marker, emit the whole thing as retry_reply. Put the orchestrator turn FIRST in the attempt's turns list, before the worker turns.
7. session_id for the attempt = the sessionID of its REAL events (first real event's sessionID). rc = the LAST match of ^\[ringer\.py\] attempt \d+ exited rc=(-?\d+)$ within the segment's real region (ignore any embedded 'exited' lines that fell before the real events). outcome: 'failed' if any real error turn or rc not in (0, None-with-stop); 'success' if the last real step_finish reason=='stop' and rc in (0, None); else 'running' when there is no real 'exited' trailer.
8. Top-level: sessions = distinct attempt session_ids in order; status = last attempt's outcome; tokens_total = last attempt's tokens_final['total'] or 0.

HOW TO VERIFY (this exact command is your check — run it yourself from the worktree root, repeatedly, until it prints PASS): sh /home/ajo/ringer/scripts/checks/transcript_parser_check.sh
The check parses real fixture logs (a happy single-attempt log, the retry log with the embedded-reproduction trap, and a mock non-JSON log) and asserts: correct attempt counts and outcomes, the spec/retry_reply orchestrator turns, tool turns carrying BOTH input and output, exact tokens_total on the happy log, and — the ACID TEST — that attempt 2's worker turns ALL carry attempt-2's session id (…lNLOee95) and none of the embedded attempt-1 events (…8GsYswD5) leak in, plus exactly 3 real tool turns in attempt 2. It also feeds a truncated log and requires status 'running' with no crash.

HARD RULES: stdlib only; create ONLY scripts/transcript.py; never git commit; do not modify the check, the fixtures, or any other file; the parser must never raise on malformed/truncated input (collect parse_warnings instead).
tasks.check: mkdir -p /tmp/ringer-transcript/patches && git add -A && git diff --cached > /tmp/ringer-transcript/patches/transcript.patch && sh /home/ajo/ringer/scripts/checks/transcript_parser_check.sh
tasks.verified: scripts/transcript.py parses raw worker.logs into orchestrator/worker turn transcripts, correctly excluding the embedded prior-attempt reproduction on retries (verified against real happy/retry/mock/truncated fixtures by scripts/checks/transcript_parser_check.sh)
